107172.form: t have immune cells that remember pathogens like animals do, so the effect is the result of switching on general protective mechanisms rather than specific ones that result from a vaccine. The team is now trying to work out the precise mechanisms in bananas, with the aim of finding ways to permanently turn them on without exposing plants to a live fungal disease. Even if it can be done, this, or other approaches such as genetic engineering, won’t solve all the industry’s problems, says Kema, not least because TR4 is far from the only disease affecting bananas.
